acceptanceState: Verified newsletter issue extraction readback is accepted as a blocked-preflight slice, not done, not accepted as full SOURCE-BROWSER-AGENTIC-RUNTIME-001 completion, and not marked done. The newsletter issue extractor is read-only archive plumbing that can run after confirmed subscription evidence; live signup, confirmation-link clicking, recurring scheduling, source-stack scoring writeback, free-community extraction, MyICOR auth, downloads, purchases, posting, messaging, and full God Mode remain pending approval-bound continuation work.
whatChanged: Added a bounded newsletter issue extraction readback command and wired it into source-session prep after confirmation readback.
whatItDoes: Once a newsletter subscription is confirmed in the shared Gmail archive, the runner reads existing Gmail thread artifacts, extracts issue rows, implementation ideas, resource links, paid-gate signals, unsubscribe/park signals, and a creator source-stack update payload without clicking links or mutating the mailbox.
whyItMatters: YouTube has discovered creator newsletter sources. This closes the next code-only gap after form detection and confirmation readback so approved newsletters can become real Foundation source evidence instead of a dead-end signup status.
whereItLives: lib/creator-newsletter-intake-runner.js issue extraction/readback builder and evaluator
lib/source-session-readiness-readback.js newsletter issue extraction queue check
proofStatus: Focused proof dogfoods waiting-for-confirmation, confirmed issue extraction, public repo link capture, no-click boundaries, no external signup, no mailbox/backlog mutation, and source-session queue visibility.
reviewNext: After Steve approves a real newsletter signup and confirmation readback finds subscribed evidence, run newsletter:issue-extraction on that exact source, then build source-stack value scoring/writeback and downstream public/repo/community handoffs.
knownLimits: This reads existing shared Gmail archive artifacts only.

This does not submit live newsletter forms, click confirmation links, click newsletter links, mutate Gmail labels, unsubscribe, download resources, purchase products, create accounts, write backlog, call providers/models, post, comment, message, or mutate credentials.
This does not make newsletters fully autonomous until live signup approval, confirmation readback, scheduling, source-stack writeback, and low-value unsubscribe/park policy are built.
